caveat: A trained readout head is NOT the LM. Design-B success means the info CAN be made recoverable at the LM boundary by a cheap merger -- the go/no-go flips to 'go' and the fine-tune question REOPENS -- it does NOT prove the LM zero-shot uses those symbols or that the economics win. Necessary, not sufficient. If Design B stays well above budget (RS budget and/or the stock frozen-merger baseline), the negative result is STRONGER than Step 0's own merged-probe fail: even a cheaply-trained, code-aware merger can't carry the signal -- scoped to Qwen2.5-VL.
